title: Supergravity flows, wormholes and their pseudo-Hermitian holographic duals
authors: Jose M. Begines, Antón F. Faedo, Carlos Hoyos, Daniele Musso
abstract: We find solutions to consistent truncations of supergravity where some real scalars are analytically extended to imaginary values, ensuring the metric remains real-valued. Among the solutions there are Lorentzian traversable wormholes connecting two asymptotically Anti-de Sitter spacetimes and flows that have a real metric also when uplifted to ten or eleven dimensions. We argue that the holographic duals are pseudo-Hermitian and $PT$-symmetric theories. Wormhole solutions also admit an interpretation as the low-energy theory of two stacks of branes and antibranes after tachyon condensation. The wormhole is then dual to an entangled state of two copies of the theory that lives on a stack of branes. We present some evidence by computing the mutual information between the theories at each boundary and by identifying the Goldstone bosons associated to the breaking of the two copies of Poincaré symmetry to their diagonal subgroup.
summary: This work explores supergravity solutions featuring traversable wormholes, interpreting them holographically as dual to entangled states of quantum field theories. The key theoretical insight is that these dual theories exhibit pseudo-Hermitian and PT-symmetric properties. The methodology combines advanced techniques from general relativity, string theory, and quantum information theory.
main_problem: The paper investigates supergravity flows and wormhole solutions, focusing on their holographic duals which are hypothesized to be pseudo-Hermitian and PT-symmetric.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that wormhole solutions can be interpreted as the low-energy theory of branes/anti-branes after tachyon condensation, dual to an entangled state of two copies of the boundary QFT.
method: The analysis combines analytic continuation of supergravity solutions, stability analysis of fluctuations around domain walls, and calculating mutual information for the dual quantum field theories.
model_or_system: The model involves consistent truncations of supergravity theories, leading to domain wall solutions connecting asymptotically Anti-de Sitter (AdS) spacetimes. The dual system is a quantum field theory exhibiting pseudo-Hermiticity and PT-symmetry.
key_observables: Mutual information, Goldstone bosons, the metric remaining real-valued after analytic extension, and the spectrum of metric fluctuations ($M^2$).
important_parameters: The periodicity of the superpotential $W(\eta)$ (e.g., $2\pi$), the separation $X$ between brane stacks, and the radial coordinate $r$ or $\eta$.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the dual theory is best understood when the metric remains real, and it relies on linearizing fluctuations around background solutions.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the formation of wormholes via tachyon condensation connecting two throats, and the general structure of the effective action and potential governing the dynamics.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by studying analytic extensions of solutions, interpreting wormholes via tachyon condensation, arguing for the pseudo-Hermitian dual, and concluding with stability analysis of fluctuations.
why_interesting: The connection between gravitational phenomena (wormholes, supergravity) and the mathematical structure of PT-symmetry and pseudo-Hermiticity provides a rich theoretical framework relevant to non-Hermitian quantum mechanics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Possible formation of the wormhole. Two stacks of branes and anti-branes form throats inside an asymptotically flat spacetime when they backreact. Condensation of the open string tachyon makes the two throats connect forming a wormhole. In the near-horizon limit the geometry is restricted to the interior of the wormhole, that connects two asymptotically AdS spacetimes.

Figure 2. Mutual information of a strip I(A)/(CV (T d−2)) as a function of the width ∆ℓ(A)/L, with C = L/Gd+1. The cases represented here are the wormholes obtained from the analytic extensions of: 1. Coulomb branch of N = 4 SYM (blue), 2. Coulomb branch of ABJM (red), and 5. GPPZ (green). The dots are computed numerically using (4.25) and (4.26), and the dashed lines are linear fits at large values of the width. The slopes of the fits agree with (4.30).
